data.title: ASCII Tennis Racket
data.solution:
import sys
import math

# Initialization
w, h, g = map(int, input().split())

# Racket Grid
print("o"*(w*2+3))
for y in range(h):
  print(end="o ")
  for x in range(w):
    print(end="| ")
  print(end="o")
  print()
  if y!=h-1:
    print(end="o-")
    for x in range(w):
      print(end="+-")
    print(end="o")
    print()
print("o"*(w*2+3))

# Grip
for i in range(g):
  if i==g-1:
    print(" "*w+"|_|")
  else:
    print(" "*w+"| |")
data.statement:
<<Goal:>> Make a rectanglular tennis racket out of ASCII characters using the given dimensions.

The racket is defined by three integers: [[w]], [[h]], and [[g]].
[[w]] = # of string columns in the grid
[[h]] = # of rows in the grid
[[g]] = length of the grip

<<Racket Head:>>
The racket head is a closed frame made of {{o}} characters with an alternating grid. The string pattern is a grid made of {{|}}, {{-}}, {{+}}, and whitespace characters. (ahhhh I don't know how to write the rest of the statement)

<<Grip:>>
The grip should be positioned at the bottom center of the racket head. You'll need to find a formula that adds enough leading whitespaces before the grip. The bottom of the racket should be {{|_|}}, and anything in between (e.g. [[g]] > 1) should be {{| |}}. Do not add trailing spaces to the end of the grip.
<<Note:>> If [[g]] = {{0}}, then do not add a grip to the racket. Unfortunately, no one will use it, but getting 100% on the test cases is more important than making usable rackets.

Good luck!

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

Goal: Make a rectanglular tennis racket out of ASCII characters using the given dimensions.

The racket is defined by three integers: w, h, and g.
w = # of string columns in the grid
h = # of rows in the grid
g = length of the grip

Racket Head:
The racket head is a closed frame made of o characters with an alternating grid. The string pattern is a grid made of |, -, +, and whitespace characters. (ahhhh I don't know how to write the rest of the statement)

Grip:
The grip should be positioned at the bottom center of the racket head. You'll need to find a formula that adds enough leading whitespaces before the grip. The bottom of the racket should be |_|, and anything in between (e.g. g > 1) should be | |. Do not add trailing spaces to the end of the grip.
Note: If g = 0, then do not add a grip to the racket. Unfortunately, no one will use it, but getting 100% on the test cases is more important than making usable rackets.

Good luck!
Input
Line 1: 3 space-separated integers w, h, and g, with w representing the # of string columns in the grid, h representing the # of rows in the grid, and g representing the length of the grip.
Output
The tennis racket after following the steps above.
Constraints
1 ≤ w ≤ 10
1 ≤ h ≤ 10
0 ≤ g ≤ 20
Example
Input
3 3 2
Output
ooooooooo
o | | | o
o-+-+-+-o
o | | | o
o-+-+-+-o
o | | | o
ooooooooo
   | |
   |_|
